scores.correctness_notes: The agent answers 'how to write a provider charmlib from scratch' rather than 'how to use existing charmlibs to implement a provider'. The gold standard emphasises finding existing interface libraries (PyPI packages vs Charmhub-hosted), using the interface libraries listing, and implementing the provider behavioural contract from the interface specification. The agent omits Juju Secrets guidance and the ecosystem context entirely. The implementation details shown are generally correct (relation events, databag writing, leader guard for app databag), though the gold standard specifically says to write to the application databag for responses, while the agent also writes to unit databags.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — all APIs and patterns shown (ops.framework.Object, ObjectEvents, EventBase, EventSource, RelationRole.provides, charm.on[relation_name], LIBID/LIBAPI/LIBPATCH constants) are real; the validation helper is defined locally, not claimed as a framework API; TooManyRelatedAppsError is imported but unused (minor sloppiness, not hallucination)
